# **ads**

### view

## ADS

## Current trends in nanocatalysis for green chemistry and its applications- a mini-review

#### Abstract

Present times are confronting many environmental, atmosphere, and energy-related issues. Efficient catalysts and eco-friendly materials could provide apt solutions for such problems. Technological advances, especially in nano-based advanced catalysis, have offered new opportunities. The applications of nanocatalysts have drawn global attention due to their tiny size and larger surface area, providing better interface and expanded functioning. A significant increase in research on the size and properties of nanomaterials led to a paradigm shift in using materials with superior activity and selectivity as catalysts. Nanocatalysis being affordable and eco-friendly, is becoming a popular option. Therefore, there is a greater necessity to comprehend nanocatalysts' remarkable properties and applications. This review focuses on their explicit properties due to their size, structure, and applications to reduce environmental issues.

[10.1016/j.cogsc.2023.100788](/link_gateway/2023COGSC..4100788A/doi:10.1016/j.cogsc.2023.100788)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Nanocatalysts, with their high surface area and unique properties, enhance catalytic activity and selectivity, enabling greener processes in chemical synthesis, thus addressing environmental challenges.

Single-atom catalysts (SACs) maximize atom efficiency, offering improved performance and stability for reactions, revolutionizing the development of catalysts with unprecedented activity and specificity.
Research emphasizes sustainable catalysts that minimize waste and energy consumption, promoting eco-friendly methodologies in chemical manufacturing while aligning with global sustainability goals.

Emerging technologies, such as AI and real-time monitoring, are transforming catalytic research, facilitating the design of advanced catalysts and optimized processes for enhanced efficiency and effectiveness.
